title: Linear regression
short: Fitting the best straight line ŷ = a + bx through two-variable data by least squares.
full:
Linear regression finds the straight line ŷ = a + bx that best predicts y from x by minimizing the sum of squared vertical distances to the points (least squares).

The slope is b = Sxy/Sxx and the intercept is a = ȳ − b·x̄, where Sxy and Sxx are the sums of cross-products and squared x-deviations. The line passes through the mean point (x̄, ȳ) and lets you predict y for a new x within the data's range.
